market_overview: Bursa Malaysia is witnessing a high-velocity rotation into Technology and Infrastructure. The semiconductor sector, led by Inari's massive RM1.80 breakout, is the primary theme today, supported by significant data centre contract flow in the construction space.

top_picks.name: INARI
top_picks.analysis: Inari has surged +13.19% on 2.77x relative volume, crushing the RM1.80 resistance. The termination of the Lumileds deal is being cheered by the market as it removes an earnings overhang.

deep_dive.thesis: Inari is entering a new bullish phase as it pivots back to its core high-margin RF and fiber segments. The market has re-rated the stock following the strategic decision to abandon the Lumileds acquisition.

top_picks.name: IJM
top_picks.analysis: IJM is riding the Data Centre boom with a new RM658m contract win. The order book is now a massive RM9.1b, with 43% focused on digital infrastructure.

deep_dive.thesis: IJM has successfully transitioned into a premier data centre builder. The rejection of the Sunway takeover offer at RM3.15 signals that the board (and market) values the company significantly higher.
catalysts: Sunway offer rejection confirms intrinsic value
risks: Rising raw material costs in construction

top_picks.name: GBGAQRS
top_picks.analysis: Showing signs of a bottom-reversal (+6.25%) on 2.2x volume. Focus is on project handovers (LRT3) which will significantly improve cash flow in mid-2026.

deep_dive.thesis: Gabungan AQRS is a classic laggard value play. As major legacy projects like LRT3 reach completion, the release of retention sums will repair the balance sheet.
catalysts: Potential new order book replenishment in 2H2026
Trading at a steep discount to book value
risks: Dependence on new contract wins post-LRT3

top_picks.name: FRONTKN
top_picks.analysis: Benefiting from the spillover interest in Inari. Technicals show a steady +2.08% climb on healthy 1.6x volume.

deep_dive.thesis: Frontken provides precision cleaning services for the chip industry and is a lower-beta way to play the semi-conductor recovery.
catalysts: Expansion in Singapore and Taiwan nodes
risks: Sensitivity to global wafer shipment volumes

top_picks.name: 99SMART
top_picks.analysis: Strong defensive rebound (+3.52%) from psychological support levels. Retail demand remains sticky despite inflationary pressures.

deep_dive.thesis: 99 Speedmart is the premier recession-proof play on Bursa. Its high-frequency retail model generates consistent cash flow regardless of macro cycles.

top_picks.name: VITROX
top_picks.analysis: Up +1.63% on high volume (2.46x). Institutional rebalancing into high-quality ATE (Automated Test Equipment) makers is evident.

deep_dive.thesis: ViTrox is a long-term compounder. While current growth is moderate, the transition to AI-integrated inspection systems is a multi-year tailwind.
risks: Premium P/E valuation makes it sensitive to earnings misses

top_picks.name: MAHSING
top_picks.analysis: Stable price action (+1.9%). Benefiting from the Johor property theme and the expansion into the industrial/data centre land segments.

deep_dive.thesis: Mah Sing is no longer just a residential developer. Its quick turnaround model and recent pivot to Industrial parks provide higher margin visibility.

top_picks.name: MRDIY
top_picks.analysis: Currently facing slight selling pressure (-2.08%). Investors are waiting for the impact of subsidy reforms on consumer spending to be fully priced in.

deep_dive.thesis: Wait and see. MR DIY has strong fundamentals, but the consumer sector is currently a laggard compared to Tech/Construction.
catalysts: Potential earnings beat in upcoming quarter

top_picks.name: ARMADA
top_picks.analysis: Energy sector is currently consolidating. Armada is trading flat (-2.4%) awaiting the next major FPSO contract announcement.

deep_dive.thesis: High-risk, high-reward. The stock needs a major contract win (e.g. in Angola or Indonesia) to re-rate from current levels.
risks: Technical breakdown on old FPSO vessels

top_picks.name: CYPARK
top_picks.analysis: Highly volatile (+16% move on low base). Lack of fundamental clarity makes it a purely speculative trading play for now.

deep_dive.thesis: Avoid for serious portfolios. The business requires significant restructuring and new RE project financing before it becomes investable.

top_picks.name: PMETAL
top_picks.analysis: Sharp sell-off (-16.6%) on extreme volume (6.0x). Technical structure is broken; avoid until a clear floor is established.

deep_dive.thesis: Wait for dust to settle. Global aluminum prices and rising carbon tax concerns are weighing on the regional smelting giant.
